title: Arbitrary Mechanics of Tang
wikitext:
[]: Arbitrary Mechanics of Tang             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Jun 16, 2013 9:46 pm Somebody brought up something called the "Dead person that you love" theory in another thread, and I was inclined to comment on it. Before everybody tangs out in EoE, they see somebody they love. Aoba was the exception, but he's not important for this. Hyuga sees Misato, Fuyutsuki sees Yui, and Maya sees Ritsuko. All of the characters that appear in front of these three are dead. Now, does somebody have to be dead to appear? In the post that I'm commenting on, the rule seems to be mentioned with the thought that only the dead can appear. I think that it doesn't matter, that the countless Reis are simply taking the shape of a loved one. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sun Jun 16, 2013 9:54 pm One of the major questions is whether the person has to have died at any point in the distant or recent past, regardless of whether the person being tanged is aware of their death, or if the person being tanged has to know their Transition Guide is dead in order to see them. This is evident in the cases of Maya and Hyuuga, since it is arguable whether either was aware that their Transition Guides (Misato and Ritsuko, respectively) were deceased at the time. However, it just so happens that every Transition Guide we see is deceased in some way or other. It might just be a coincidence, although it's impossible to tell since we only see some of the Nerv staff Transition Guides, and most of Nerv was dead at the time. Note that in some shots we just see Rei standing over the person, such as in the cases of Ritsuko, Misato and the dead Nerv soldiers.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Jun 16, 2013 9:56 pm It's completely up in the air. You can easily read it as coincidence that all three are dead, and I'd say it's a stretch to call Yui "dead" to begin with.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Jun 16, 2013 9:59 pm Shinoyami65 wrote: Note that in some shots we just see Rei standing over the person, such as in the cases of Ritsuko, Misato and the dead Nerv soldiers.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sun Jun 16, 2013 10:04 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: For the dead Nerv staff, I like to think that nobody special is appearing because they can't picture anybody to appear. In the case of Misato, I'm not sure what to think. She obviously had a person she loved- Kaji, in this case. As far as Ritsuko's situation, I don't believe that she loved anybody at the moment that she died. But back to Misato, you're right; it's a little odd how nobody appears before her, save Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Jun 16, 2013 10:07 pm I made a long post about this once. The gist of it was that Quantum Rei transforms into the person that the "tangee" loves/trusts most. It's a gentler way of getting the person to let down his/her AT Field so that the soul can escape and go back to the Chamber of Guf. Aoba apparently had no one, so Rei just took his soul by force. EDIT: @Shinoyami I can't believe I missed that! That perfectly explains Misato's final line: "Kaji-kun, I did the right thing, didn't I?" The camera then pans out to show Quantum Rei floating right next to her. Also, I just went through that scene frame by frame and found out that you actually see Misato's body get torn in half by the explosion....

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Jun 16, 2013 10:13 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: For the dead Nerv staff, I like to think that nobody special is appearing because they can't picture anybody to appear. In the case of Misato, I'm not sure what to think. She obviously had a person she loved- Kaji, in this case. As far as Ritsuko's situation, I don't believe that she loved anybody at the moment that she died. But back to Misato, you're right; it's a little odd how nobody appears before her, save Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sun Jun 16, 2013 10:15 pm The real mystery here is Aoba's Reiquarium assault. I mean, if Rei can simply appear in her schoolgirl form, why go to all the trouble of traumatising the poor guy with the Reiquarium clones? Possibly the AT Field is weakened by trauma as well as by interaction with trusted individuals, thus Rei was forced to take the hard route because Aoba doesn't trust or love anyone to a great extent? As for Misato, who knows? She was talking to both Kaji and Pen-Pen at the end. Note that Rei also noticeably appears to Ritsuko (who does see her) right after she gets shot, but before Lilith manifests the Anti-ATF and tangs Ritsuko's corpse. So it's possible that Misato could also see something.

[]:             Quote Post by VirtualBathsalt » Sun Jun 16, 2013 11:27 pm I feel like I'd need to rewatch EoE to say for certain, but could it be possible that Misato and Ritsuko don't have the same experience because the tanging/anti-AT field simply had not yet begun when they expired?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jun 17, 2013 1:49 am I suppose it would make a certain amount of sense if the Rei-tachi could only appear in the form of someone they've already collected. I still like the idea however that what a person sees, if anything, is a product of their own mind trying to rationalize the experience of becoming tang rather than something Rei/Lilith is actually doing on purpose. The sensation of having one's ATF penetrated triggers hallucinations of whomever or whatever came the closest to doing that metaphorically in that person's life. Actually, makes me wonder if the Angels experience something like that when an eva cancels out their ATF mid-combat. Between that sensation and the evas being made in Adam's image that's gotta be surreal even for them.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Mon Jun 17, 2013 2:01 am NemZ wrote: Actually, makes me wonder if the Angels experience something like that when an eva cancels out their ATF mid-combat. Between that sensation and the evas being made in Adam's image that's gotta be surreal even for them.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Jun 17, 2013 2:10 am This is important- we never see what the anti at field looks like from the perspective of the random soldiers, or the dead Nerv personnel that liquefy in the hallway. We only see her standing over them. Her appearance there is not for their benefit but for ours, so that we understand fully what's going on, that this is a universal phenomenon. Maya, Hyuga and Fuyutsuki never comment on Rei- Hyuga is too busy being terrified and then euphoric and Fuyutsuki addresses Yui directly. Seeing Rei there is again for our benefit.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Jun 17, 2013 11:39 am NemZ wrote: The sensation of having one's ATF penetrated triggers hallucinations of whomever or whatever came the closest to doing that metaphorically in that person's life.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Jun 17, 2013 5:40 pm Uhhhh.... I'm contractually obligated to bring up the last sorry thread to deal with this issue , the entirety of which is entirely my fault. I'm taking the opportunity to officially disown any and all arguments I made in that thread, constituting as they do an intolerable level of over-analysis. The whole previous thread can be summarized (with far less drama) by the material in the 12 or so post already in this thread. We're observing blatantly out of character behavior for all three transition guides concern: Misato going for Hyuuga -- despite known an desperate pinings for Kaji (and Shinji), and Ritusko as a Lesbian. The only guide that makes any real sense is Yui, assuming you believe that Yui would make time for Fuyutsuki like this -- I do -- but it does not follow that Misato and Ritsuko would or even could do the same. The case of Aoba is peculiar in that it can be both dismissed, and yet simultaneously clarifies the entire scene. If he had instead been tanged by a mass of floating guitars, or images of rock stars, everything could make a lot more sense. But as it happens he just sees a multitude of Rei's. This could be taken to mean that Aoba has "seen past" the (ATF based?) illusions of the Rei's. On the other hand, as has already been noted, everyone we see is "dead" for a given meaning of that term. At the least, we can say that everyone seen is in some way or the other Complemented. It could simply be that the Rei's, as Lilith's giggling avatar-ettes, have access to the memories/forms of the recently tanged. But then again, they seem to have access to the minds and desires of the living as well -- Aoba excepted for some reason. I'm inclined to think that the Yui we see really is Yui, giving Fuyutsuki a send off. But the nature of what is seen by the Bridge Bunnies proper is more difficult to pin-down.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Jun 17, 2013 7:24 pm It's cute in fanfic and all but it's lame and kind of sexist to presume that Maya's affection for Ritsuko is homosexual because she shot down Kaji once. The three people who's POV we're exposed during the beginning of the tangification could easily illustrate different types of love/affection. Just because Hyuga saw a sex object doesn't mean everyone did, including Fuyutsuki and Maya. We're clearly shown Rei transforming into the transition guides. What a person sees as they discorporate is from their own mind, hence the out of character behavior. Fuyutsuki couldn't have been seeing Yui, she was in the Eva and remained there. This is critical to the themes of the film- Instrumentality is a fantasy world, a false hope. In any case, what we see with the transition guides has nothing to do with love or sex at all. It has to do with desire . In each case, we see a fantasy being fulfilled. In some cases, this coincides with sexual attraction, not necessarily all. Hyuga wants Misato to approach him sexually. She does. Fuyutsuki wants to see Yui again. He does. Maya wants Ritsuko to show her respect and affection. She does. Aoba is probably the most mentally healthy person on the bridge- he wants to live , so he has a nightmare vision until his defenses are battered down by overwhelming brute force. These relationships are all unrequited, as well, even if we discount the romantic angle for some of them. Again, it's a commentary on loss and suffering, which defines who we are as human beings and is an essential part of the human experience. We're nothing without it.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Jun 17, 2013 7:57 pm I'd have to agree with almost the entity of the above, particularly the part about Instrumentality as a false hope being in keeping with the theme of the film. However, as usual in Eva, there is a spanner in the worths. Chuckman wrote: Fuyutsuki couldn't have been seeing Yui, she was in the Eva and remained there.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon Jun 17, 2013 8:52 pm Chuckman wrote: It's cute in fanfic and all but it's lame and kind of sexist to presume that Maya's affection for Ritsuko is homosexual because she shot down Kaji once.

[]:             Quote Post by Joy Evangelion » Mon Jun 17, 2013 10:39 pm Should Shinji's tanging be considered here, or is this discussion just concerning the bridge bunnies and Fuyutsuki? I know Shinji doesn't have a transition guide like the others, and his case might be different because he's in Unit-01, but it's interesting to note that right before he gets that big smile on his face and sees the down-shirt shot of Asuka and tangs he's actually asked "What do you wish for?"(just one translation, though). Going off that a little bit, I think that what the characters are seeing before tanging is what they desire or wish for, and although their desires don't have to be sexual, I think they just basically turn out to be sexual anyway(except for Aoba, of course). [/spoiler]

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jun 17, 2013 10:43 pm perhaps more interesting, should the Gendo that Rei sees before she dies in 23 be considered as well?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jun 17, 2013 10:47 pm Joy Evangelion wrote: Should Shinji's tanging be considered here, or is this discussion just concerning the bridge bunnies and Fuyutsuki?
